findings.description: Stored XSS: display_name/action/created_at are f-string-interpolated into <td> cells with zero HTML escaping, so a user-controlled display_name like <script>...</script> executes in the admin's browser when the report is viewed.
The caller-supplied title is injected unescaped into <title> and <h1> (lines 11/13), giving a second unescaped HTML sink and XSS vector alongside the row data.

Docstring claims the range [start, end] includes both boundary days, but the query uses 'created_at < end' (half-open), silently dropping every event on the final day — a contract-violating off-by-one.
fetch_events applies no LIMIT and the whole result set plus the entire HTML string are held in memory while rows are built, so a large date range OOMs/degrades with no streaming.
Building 'rows' via repeated 'rows += ...' string concatenation in the loop is O(n^2) reallocation; a list-join (or generator) is the standard fix.

Date filtering relies on lexicographic string comparison of date.isoformat() against created_at, so correctness is silently coupled to an ISO timestamp storage format and breaks for any non-ISO/locale date column.
Every DB method opens a fresh connection with no error handling, so a locked/missing/corrupt sqlite file raises a raw OperationalError to the admin surface with no graceful degradation.
save_report performs file I/O with no exception handling, so permission/disk-full/bad-path errors propagate raw with no contextual message.
PAGE_TEMPLATE.format(title=...) will raise KeyError/ValueError if title ever contains a stray '{' or '}' brace, an avoidable fragility versus a non-reparsing substitution.
